message:
perf(triage): introduce TriageLookupCache to avoid repeated graph traversals

Add a TriageLookupCache struct that precomputes three frequently-accessed
lookups once at the start of compute_triage() instead of recomputing them
per-issue:

- issue_by_id: HashMap<&str, &Issue> for O(1) issue lookups by ID,
  replacing linear scans in quick_wins filtering.
- open_blocker_count_by_issue: HashMap<&str, usize> caching
  graph.open_blockers() results, which previously traversed adjacency
  lists on every call to compute_impact_score() and
  compute_blockers_to_clear().
- issues_in_cycles: HashSet<&str> flattening the Vec<Vec<String>> cycle
  data into a single membership set, replacing the O(cycles * cycle_len)
  .iter().any(|cycle| cycle.contains(...)) check per issue.

The cache is threaded through compute_impact_score() and
compute_blockers_to_clear() via a shared reference, replacing the
previous IssueGraph parameter where only blocker counts were needed.

This reduces the triage hot path from O(n * m) graph traversals to O(n)
lookups for an n-issue repository with m average blockers per issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
